page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-384GB is a KVM-hypervised virtual server configured with 64 vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM. The underlying hardware features an AMD EPYC 7542 processor operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. This configuration provides 64 physical cores with a single thread per core, resulting in a memory-to-core ratio of 6.0 GB per core. The CPU features 16 MB of L3 cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 64 KB of L1d cache, with support for AVX, AES, and SHA_NI instruction sets. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark data reveals a clear tradeoff between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core execution is relatively weak, with stress-ng single-core scoring in the bottom 10% and single-threaded gzip and bzip3 compression tests falling into the bottom 25%. However, the server excels in multi-threaded and memory-intensive operations. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, PassMark CPU tests, and Redis SET operations all register in the top 25% of benchmarks. Memory bandwidth is also highly competitive, with small-block cached reads and large-block uncached writes scoring in the top 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU allocation model allows for a high density of resources at a lower qualitative cost profile compared to dedicated-core instances of similar scale. This makes the server highly suitable for memory-heavy, parallelizable workloads that do not require sustained peak single-core performance. Appropriate workloads include multi-threaded database operations, large-scale caching layers like Redis, parallel software compilation, and moderate-scale LLM prompt processing.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-384GB is a KVM-hypervised virtual server powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor on the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 vCPUs with shared CPU allocation, 384.0 GB of RAM, and no bundled local storage or GPUs. Performance benchmarks highlight a distinct tradeoff: single-core execution is weak, ranking in the bottom 10% for stress-ng, whereas multi-threaded operations and memory bandwidth are highly competitive. Specifically, PassMark CPU tests, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Redis SET operations all score in the top 25%. With 6.0 GB of RAM per core, this server offers a cost-effective resource profile for memory-intensive, highly parallelized workloads. It is well-suited for large-scale caching, database operations, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-384GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 384 GB of RAM, offering strong multi-threaded performance and high memory bandwidth for parallel workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-64xCPU-384GB is a KVM virtual server with 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 384 GB of RAM, optimized for multi-threaded and memory-heavy tasks.
tagline: A high-capacity virtual server balancing sixty-four AMD EPYC cores with three hundred eighty-four gigabytes of RAM for parallelized, memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 64 AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs and 384.0 GB of system memory
Shared CPU allocation on KVM hypervisor with x86_64 architecture
Strong multi-threaded performance and high memory write bandwidth
Weak single-core performance offset by excellent parallel scaling
Best suited for large-scale caching, databases, and multi-threaded processing
